0:["Tcfe-T2cr6zJuT9Ir7N5Q",[[["",{"children":["blog",{"children":["meal-prep-muscle",{"children":["__PAGE__",{}]}]}]},"$undefined","$undefined",true],["",{"children":["blog",{"children":["meal-prep-muscle",{"children":["__PAGE__",{},[["$L1",["$","main",null,{"className":"min-h-screen bg-slate-950","children":[["$","script",null,{"type":"application/ld+json","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"{\"@context\":\"https://schema.org\",\"@type\":\"Article\",\"headline\":\"How to Meal Prep for Muscle Gain\",\"author\":{\"@type\":\"Organization\",\"name\":\"GetFitAI\",\"url\":\"https://www.getfitai.io\"},\"publisher\":{\"@type\":\"Organization\",\"name\":\"GetFitAI\"},\"datePublished\":\"2025-04-28\",\"dateModified\":\"2025-04-28\"}"}}],["$","article",null,{"className":"pt-32 pb-20 px-4 sm:px-6","children":["$","div",null,{"className":"max-w-3xl mx-auto","children":[["$","header",null,{"className":"mb-12","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-3 mb-4","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"px-3 py-1 bg-orange-500/10 text-orange-400 text-xs font-medium rounded-full","children":"Nutrition"}],["$","span",null,{"className":"text-sm text-slate-500","children":"Apr 28, 2025"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"relative w-full h-64 md:h-80 rounded-2xl overflow-hidden mb-8","children":["$","img",null,{"src":"/images/blog/meal-prep.jpg","alt":"Meal prep containers with healthy food","className":"w-full h-full object-cover grayscale"}]}],["$","h1",null,{"className":"text-4xl md:text-5xl font-bold text-white mb-6 leading-tight","children":"How to Meal Prep for Muscle Gain"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-slate-400 leading-relaxed","children":"Save time, eat better, and build muscle with these simple meal prep strategies designed for hypertrophy."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose prose-lg max-w-none prose-headings:text-white prose-p:text-slate-400 prose-strong:text-white prose-li:text-slate-400","children":[["$","h2",null,{"children":"Why Meal Prep Matters for Muscle Gain"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Building muscle requires consistent nutrition. You need enough protein to repair tissue, enough carbs to fuel training, and enough calories to support growth. When you're scrambling for meals at the last minute, you default to whatever is convenient—and convenience rarely aligns with muscle-building goals."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Meal prep removes decision fatigue. When your food is already cooked and portioned, you simply eat what's planned. This consistency is what separates those who make progress from those who spin their wheels."}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"The Muscle-Building Meal Formula"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Every meal should include:"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Protein:"}]," 30-40g per meal (palm-sized portion of meat, fish, eggs, or tofu)"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Carbs:"}]," 1-2 cupped handfuls (rice, potatoes, oats, fruit)"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Fats:"}]," 1 thumb-sized portion (olive oil, nuts, avocado, egg yolks)"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Vegetables:"}]," 2 fist-sized portions (for micronutrients and fiber)"]}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Step 1: Plan Your Menu"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Pick 2-3 protein sources, 2-3 carb sources, and 2 fat sources for the week. Variety is nice, but simplicity scales. Most successful meal preppers rotate the same 4-6 meals."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Example week:"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Proteins:"}]," Chicken breast, ground beef, salmon, eggs"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Carbs:"}]," White rice, sweet potatoes, oats"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Fats:"}]," Olive oil, almonds, avocado"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Veggies:"}]," Broccoli, spinach, bell peppers"]}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Step 2: Shop Once, Cook Once"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Go grocery shopping on Sunday. Buy everything you need for the week in one trip. Then spend 2-3 hours cooking and portioning."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Batch cook your proteins in the oven or slow cooker. Roast vegetables on sheet pans. Cook a large pot of rice. While everything cooks, portion last week's meals into containers."}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Step 3: Portion and Store"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Invest in glass or BPA-free plastic containers. Portion each meal into individual containers so you can grab and go. Label containers with the date if you're prepping more than 3-4 days ahead."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Most prepped meals last 4-5 days in the fridge. Freeze anything beyond that. Soups, stews, and cooked meats freeze well. Fresh vegetables and rice do not."}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Sample Muscle-Building Meal Prep Day"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Here's what a full day of eating looks like for a 180-pound man in a lean bulk:"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Breakfast:"}]," 3 whole eggs + 3 egg whites, 1 cup oats with banana, 1 tbsp peanut butter (~700 cal, 40g protein)"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Lunch:"}]," 6 oz chicken breast, 1.5 cups rice, 1 cup broccoli, 1 tbsp olive oil (~750 cal, 50g protein)"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Snack:"}]," Greek yogurt with berries and almonds (~400 cal, 25g protein)"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Dinner:"}]," 6 oz salmon, 1 cup sweet potato, mixed salad with avocado (~700 cal, 45g protein)"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Pre-bed:"}]," Casein protein shake or cottage cheese (~200 cal, 25g protein)"]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Total:"}]," ~2,750 calories, 185g protein"]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Meal Prep Hacks"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Use a slow cooker:"}]," Set it and forget it. Perfect for shredded chicken, beef stew, and chili."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Buy pre-cut vegetables:"}]," Worth the extra cost if it saves you from skipping veggies."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Cook extra dinner:"}]," Intentionally make 2-3 extra portions for tomorrow's lunch."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Keep sauces simple:"}]," Soy sauce, hot sauce, mustard, and salsa add flavor without significant calories."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Prep snacks too:"}]," Portion nuts, cut fruit, and prep protein shakes so you're never caught hungry."]}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Common Mistakes"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Not eating enough:"}]," If you're not gaining weight, you're not eating enough. Track for one week to find your true maintenance."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Too much variety:"}]," Complex meal plans fail. Simple, repeatable meals win."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Skipping vegetables:"}]," Micronutrients matter for recovery, energy, and health."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Prepping too far ahead:"}]," Food quality degrades after day 5. Prep twice a week if needed."]}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-16 relative overflow-hidden rounded-2xl","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"absolute inset-0 bg-gradient-to-br from-orange-500 via-orange-500 to-orange-600"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"absolute inset-0 bg-[radial-gradient(ellipse_at_top_right,_var(--tw-gradient-stops))] from-yellow-400/20 via-transparent to-transparent"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"absolute inset-0 opacity-10","style":{"backgroundImage":"url(\"data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg width='60' height='60' viewBox='0 0 60 60'
